name: auth-handler
description: Manage authentication, authorization, and user sessions. Use when dealing with login, sign-up, API protection, middleware, or user data fetching.
qualityReason: Specific to Next.js and NextAuth v5
capabilities: Implement API route protection with NextAuth v5 wrapper functions
Configure authentication for standard routes, super admin routes, and cron jobs
Set up client-side authentication with SWR-based useUser hook
Implement server-side authentication checks and user data fetching
Configure NextAuth providers, adapters, and database schema
Apply defense-in-depth security patterns for authentication
author: AayushBaniya2006
securitySummary: This skill provides documentation and guidance for implementing authentication in Next.js applications. All static findings are false positives. The external command patterns are TypeScript code examples in markdown documentation, not executable code. The network and filesystem references are metadata in JSON files. No actual security risks detected.
valueStatement: Authentication implementation can be complex and error-prone, leading to security vulnerabilities. This skill provides clear guidelines for implementing secure authentication, authorization, and session management in Next.js applications using NextAuth v5 with defense-in-depth protection patterns.
userTitle: Manage Next.js Authentication with NextAuth v5
limitations: Focused specifically on Next.js applications with NextAuth v5
Assumes use of specific project structure with src directory
Does not cover authentication for other frameworks or libraries
References project-specific files that may not exist in all codebases

useCases.targetUser: Backend developers building secure Next.js APIs
useCases.title: API Route Protection
useCases.description: Protect API routes by implementing authentication wrappers for standard routes, super admin routes, and cron jobs with defense-in-depth security patterns.

useCases.targetUser: Frontend developers building authenticated user interfaces
useCases.title: Frontend Authentication State
useCases.description: Implement client-side authentication state management using SWR-based useUser hook to access current user data in React components.

useCases.targetUser: Full-stack developers implementing server-side rendering with authentication
useCases.title: Server-Side User Data Access
useCases.description: Fetch user data and check authentication status on the server side using NextAuth session management and user plan retrieval functions.

promptTemplates.title: Protect an API Route
promptTemplates.prompt: Protect this API route with authentication using withAuthRequired
promptTemplates.scenario: Adding authentication to a new API endpoint

promptTemplates.title: Add Client Authentication
promptTemplates.prompt: Add user authentication to this React component using the useUser hook
promptTemplates.scenario: Displaying user-specific data in a frontend component

promptTemplates.title: Implement Super Admin Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Protect this admin route with super admin authentication using withSuperAdminAuthRequired

promptTemplates.title: Configure NextAuth Provider
promptTemplates.prompt: Add a new OAuth provider to the NextAuth configuration in auth.ts
promptTemplates.scenario: Extending authentication to support additional login methods

bestPractices: Always implement route-level authentication wrappers in addition to middleware for defense-in-depth security
Use the useUser hook for client components instead of useSession from next-auth react library
Configure environment variables for AUTH_SECRET, provider credentials, and SUPER_ADMIN_EMAILS before deployment
antiPatterns: Relying solely on middleware for authentication without individual route protection
Using useSession from next-auth react instead of the project-specific useUser hook
Assuming session.user contains complete user data instead of fetching with getUserPlan

faq.question: What is the difference between withAuthRequired and withSuperAdminAuthRequired?
faq.answer: withAuthRequired protects routes for any authenticated user while withSuperAdminAuthRequired restricts access to users listed in the SUPER_ADMIN_EMAILS environment variable.

faq.question: Why should I use useUser instead of useSession from next-auth?
faq.answer: The useUser hook is a project-specific wrapper that uses SWR for better caching and revalidation. It provides a consistent interface for accessing user data across client components.

faq.question: How do I protect cron job endpoints?
faq.answer: Use the cronAuthRequired wrapper function specifically designed for automated task endpoints that need authentication without user sessions.

faq.question: What is defense-in-depth for authentication?
faq.answer: Defense-in-depth means implementing multiple layers of security. Use both middleware for initial route filtering and individual route wrappers for guaranteed protection at the endpoint level.

faq.question: Where do I configure OAuth providers like Google?
faq.answer: Configure OAuth providers in the src/auth.ts file. You will need to add provider credentials and set environment variables like GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET.

faq.question: How do I access full user data in API routes?
faq.answer: The session.user object contains minimal data. Use getUserPlan with the user ID to fetch complete user information including subscription plan details from the database.
